raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The median price of a home in California is roughly $800,000, which is more than double the national average.",
    "More than two in five households in California currently spend over 30% of their income on housing.",
    "More than one in five households in California spend over 50% of their income on housing.",
    "Families who pay more than 30% of their income for housing are considered rent burdened by the Department of Housing and Urban Development.",
    "California has been losing lower and middle-income residents due to the high cost of living.",
    "Over 97% of cities and counties in California have been unable to produce enough affordable housing.",
    "There are only enough affordable and available rentals for 24% of extremely low-income households in California.",
    "Social housing is defined as publicly backed, self-sustaining housing that accommodates a mix of household income ranges.",
    "Social housing is protected from being sold to a private for-profit entity for the duration of its life.",
    "Housing for people with higher incomes will subsidize low-income units, allowing housing developments to become self-sustaining and revenue neutral.",
    "Social housing aims to ensure that tenants spend no more than 30% of their income on rent.",
    "Social housing has been successful in various locations around the world, including CADA in Sacramento and developments in Vienna and Singapore."
  ]
}
```
